events.label: reasoning
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Frayed/burned webbing on fall protection equipment",
    "Webbing damage compromises tensile strength and energy absorption capacity"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove damaged equipment from service",
    "Tag as 'Do Not Use' and quarantine for disposal or repair per manufacturer",
    "Inspect all other fall protection equipment on site for similar damage",
    "Replace with compliant, undamaged equipment before allowing work at height"
  ],
  "rationale": "Frayed or burned webbing indicates structural degradation of the synthetic fibers. OSHA 1910.140(c)(18) requires that personal fall protection systems be inspected before each use and removed from service if defective. Damaged webbing can fail under load, creating a high risk of serious injury or fatality. The equipment is installed but not yet closed out, so it must be failed and replaced before work proceeds.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(18) - Inspection and removal from service",
    "OSHA 1926.502(d)(21) - Lanyards and lifelines: defective equipment removal",
    "ANSI Z359.1 - Fall protection equipment inspection criteria"
  ]
}
